title: russelia longipedunculata
intro: Russelia longipedunculata, also known as the Firecracker plant or Coral honeysuckle, is a flowering plant species in the family Boraginaceae. It is a climbing shrub native to Mexico, with a history of traditional use in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant is used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin irritations, offering benefits such as pain relief and improved immune function. Russelia longipedunculata can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, making it a versatile herb for herbal preparations.
